judge.reasoning: • SCC of 68 modules with 220 internal edges and blast_radius 1.0 means any change ripples everywhere.\n• Cycle edges confirmed in Layer 1: vlmeval.smp.vlm → vlmeval.utils → vlmeval.utils.matching_util → vlmeval.smp forming a tight loop.\n• All structural signals at maximum (principle_severity 1.0, pattern_violation 1.0) with no recent remediation (recency 0.0).
judge.dissent_summary: • Codebase may be a research/eval toolkit where monolithic coupling is an accepted trade-off.\n• Cycle is within a single top-level package, limiting cross-repo blast radius.
judge.recommended_action: • Extract shared primitives (logging, file I/O, path utils) from vlmeval/smp/ into a new vlmeval/core/ module (~100 LOC), making it dependency-free.\n• Redirect vlmeval.utils.matching_util and vlmeval.smp.vlm to import from vlmeval.core, breaking the smp↔utils cycle in one PR.\n• Audit remaining 220 intra-SCC edges in vlmeval/dataset/ and vlmeval/api/base.py to introduce one-way dependency from dataset→api→core over a multi-week effort.

judge.reasoning: • Layer 1 shows layer_drop of 5: omnilmm.train.train_utils (layer 5) imports omnilmm (layer 0).\n• blast_radius=1.0 and principle_severity=1.0 confirm maximum structural impact from this back-edge.\n• The import at omnilmm/train/train_utils.py:16 pulls `conversation` from the root package, a cross-cutting concern misplaced at layer 0.
judge.dissent_summary: • omnilmm may be a genuine cross-cutting utility, making the violation defensible.\n• recency=0.0 suggests the violation is stable and not actively worsening.
judge.recommended_action: • Extract omnilmm/conversation.py (~40 LOC) into a new omnilmm/utils/conversation.py at a lower layer.\n• Update omnilmm/train/train_utils.py line 16 to import from omnilmm.utils.conversation instead of omnilmm.\n• Remove or re-export the conversation symbol from omnilmm/__init__.py to eliminate the upward edge.

judge.reasoning: • `vlmeval.config` acts as a model registry (cross-cutting utility), not true layer-1 infrastructure.\n• `blast_radius` of 1.0 signals wide use, confirming config is a shared lookup table consumed at many layers.\n• `inference_video.py:3` imports only `supported_VLM` dict — a read-only registry lookup, not an upward orchestration call.
judge.dissent_summary: • Layer-drop of 4 (layer 5 → layer 1) is a severe architectural inversion by P6.\n• `vlmeval.config` itself imports `vlmeval.vlm` and `vlmeval.api`, making it a heavyweight dependency.
judge.recommended_action: • Extract `supported_VLM` registry dict from `vlmeval/config.py` into a new `vlmeval/registry.py` (~30 LOC).\n• Make `vlmeval/config.py` import from `vlmeval/registry.py`, and update `inference_video.py` to import from `vlmeval/registry.py`.\n• Verify no circular imports are introduced across `vlmeval/vlm` and `vlmeval/api` in one PR.

judge.reasoning: • `blast_radius: 1.0` and `recency: 0.0` indicate widespread but stable, long-standing usage.\n• `vlmeval.utils` exports only `track_progress_rich` and matching utilities — genuinely cross-cutting concerns.\n• `inference_video.py:4` imports `track_progress_rich`, a progress-display utility with no upward dependency itself.
judge.dissent_summary: • Layer drop of 4 (layer 5 → layer 1) is a severe architectural violation per P6.\n• `blast_radius: 1.0` means any change to `vlmeval.utils` affects all dependents including inference_video.
judge.recommended_action: • Annotate `vlmeval/utils/__init__.py` with an explicit cross-cutting exemption comment (~5 LOC) to document the justified violation.\n• Monitor `vlmeval.utils` for scope creep; restrict additions to stateless, side-effect-free utilities only.

judge.reasoning: • `blast_radius: 1.0` and `layer_drop: 4` in measured impact signal wide structural exposure.\n• `inference_video.py:5` uses `from vlmeval.smp import *`, a wildcard upward dependency violating P6.\n• `vlmeval.smp` re-exports file, vlm, misc, log utilities — classic cross-cutting concern misplaced at layer 1.
judge.dissent_summary: • `vlmeval.smp` acts as a cross-cutting utility layer, making the dependency pragmatically defensible.\n• `recency: 0.0` suggests the violation is stable and not actively worsening.
judge.recommended_action: • Extract shared utilities (file, misc, log) from `vlmeval/smp/__init__.py` into a new `vlmeval/utils/common.py` (~60 LOC).\n• Update `vlmeval/inference_video.py` lines 1-10 to import from `vlmeval.utils.common` instead of `vlmeval.smp`.\n• Remove the wildcard `from vlmeval.smp import *` in `inference_video.py` and replace with explicit named imports in one PR.
